task: Craft Twitter/X launch messaging for askr by identifying pain points, building social reach strategy, and finalizing tweet copy with visual
discussion_summary: User is one week from public launch of askr (a Claude session daemon that handles quota checkpoints and context limits). Session focused on Twitter strategy: moved from posting solution-focused content to building reach by engaging with relevant accounts first. Landed on sarcasm-driven tweet about repetitive Claude session handoffs (switching machines, re-explaining details) paired with Homelander meme image. User rejected generic 'one week out' teaser and weak two-problem framing—insisted on three problems and authentic voice. Final decision: post the sarcasm tweet with Homelander image, spacing TBD.

accomplishments.what: Identified three core pain points askr solves: quota management, context limits, session continuity across machines
Built curated follow list of 10 accounts (alexalbert__, simonw, swyx, karpathy, etc.) for natural reach without direct plugging
Finalized tweet copy with sarcasm opener and Homelander visual that frames problem authentically
Removed Phase 4 Public Launch section from roadmap.md (premature detail, focus on core build)

next_actions.action: Post the tweet with Homelander image to @bippin account. Decide spacing based on visual balance (spaced vs compact)—user showed both options, go with whichever reads cleaner on mobile.
next_actions.why: Tweet is finalized and ready; this is the immediate next step to start building reach before launch

next_actions.action: Follow the 10 curated accounts (alexalbert__, simonw, swyx, karpathy, amasad, emollick, garrytan, levelsio, marc_louvion, kunal0dha) to establish presence in relevant circles
next_actions.why: Enables natural engagement and reply strategy without cold outreach; builds audience correlation before askr reveal

next_actions.action: Monitor replies to the sarcasm tweet and engage authentically with anyone who resonates—don't pitch askr yet, just be the person who understands this pain
next_actions.why: Builds credibility and audience before launch; creates natural thread for eventual solution reveal

next_actions.action: Finalize remaining Phase 3.11 JSON Handover Schema work and stress-tests/ directory before public launch (one week timeline)
next_actions.why: Roadmap now reflects focus on core build; these are the last blockers before GitHub release

next_actions.action: Prepare GitHub launch assets: polished README with GIF/screenshot, clean install story, changelog, and release notes
next_actions.why: Public launch is one week out; these are table-stakes for credible GitHub debut

decisions.decision: Rejected generic 'one week out' teaser in favor of sarcasm-driven problem statement with Homelander meme
decisions.reason: Teaser makes askr look weak and narrow; sarcasm + visual establishes pattern recognition and authentic voice

decisions.decision: Removed Phase 4 Public Launch section from roadmap.md
decisions.reason: Premature detail; focus should stay on Phase 3.11 completion and stress-tests before launch week

decisions.decision: Chose reply/engagement strategy over direct posting to build reach
decisions.reason: User has zero time and new account; piggybacking on established voices (lachygroom, swyx, levelsio, marc_louvion) is only viable path to audience

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Tweet opening: 'been building the fix for both. one week out.'
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: That makes askr look weak, as that's not the only two things askr is [solving]

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Tweet opening: 'third problem I kept hitting while building with claude:'
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: starting it like that makes it gay as hell

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Generic reach strategy without curated account list
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: give me a list of people I can slap a follow onto, who will actually correlate with I'm building

failed_approaches.approach: Solution-focused teaser ('both problems I posted about? fixed. built a daemon...')
failed_approaches.reason: User rejected because askr isn't launched yet and it reveals too much; strategy should be reach-building first, solution reveal later
User correctly identified this makes askr look narrow; three problems establish pattern recognition
User has zero time and new account; engagement strategy with established voices is only viable path

relational_files.why: User checked README to ground tweet in askr's actual functionality; will need polish for GitHub launch
